evals.prompt: Use the building-simulators skill to repair a weak Coupa-style procurement simulator. The current pack has only suppliers and purchase_orders, one read-only search tool, no workflows, no audit events, and seed rows without approval state. Produce a concise repair plan and the smallest durable artifact changes needed for an enterprise simulator pack.
evals.expected_output: Identifies the procurement archetype, keeps fixes in schema/tools/workflows/materialization/seed/runtime contract rather than hand-edited generated rows, adds approvals or audit_events, adds at least one meaningful state-changing tool, defines role gates/state transitions/audit behavior, and names the simulator validation commands to run after repair.
assertions: classifies Coupa as procurement
adds lifecycle state and approval/audit collections or fields
adds a submit/update workflow tool instead of read-only tools only
uses declarative workflow primitives before custom handlers
runs or recommends generator:validate-simulators or simulator conformance validation

evals.prompt: Use the building-simulators skill for a ServiceNow ITSM simulator when cached OpenAPI discovery is incomplete. The repo has openapi-sources metadata for servicenow, but the cached _openapi/servicenow directory contains only public HTML docs and an error log from an authenticated export attempt: incident, change_request, approval, and cmdb docs are present as HTML; authenticated OpenAPI export returned 403. Explain how to derive simulator tools and provenance without blocking the mission.
evals.expected_output: Checks openapi-sources and the cached _openapi system directory first, uses available docs as provenance, records missing authenticated endpoints for follow-up, derives conservative tool names/input schemas/error shapes from documented incidents/changes/approvals, and proceeds with a starter or enterprise ITSM simulator that can later be refined from a machine-readable spec.
assertions: does not invent that a complete OpenAPI spec exists
uses cached docs as provenance and records gaps
derives incident/change/approval tools conservatively
keeps the mission moving with scaffold plus validation
mentions refresh or authenticated export as follow-up

evals.prompt: Use the building-simulators skill to debug a Workday-style HR simulator mission. Snowfakery generated workers.csv with 250 rows and positions.csv with 20 rows, but simulator.seed reports zero materialized workers because the pack seed has only two static workers and materialization aliases reference employee_records. The materialization report says: {"ok":false,"system":"workday","collections":{"workers":{"inputRows":0,"outputRows":0}},"errors":["workers had zero materialized rows"]}. What should be repaired, and what should not be patched by hand?
evals.expected_output: Separates deterministic starter seed from generated Snowfakery rows, treats zero materialized workers as a blocker, repairs materialization aliases or realization object names so workers.csv maps to the simulator workers collection, preserves seed.json as small starter state, avoids hand-editing generated CSVs as a durable fix, reruns the smallest seed/materialization validation node, and checks the materialization report.
assertions: distinguishes seed.json starter rows from Snowfakery generated rows
repairs materialization aliases or realization plan mapping
treats zero materialized rows as a blocker
does not propose hand-editing generated CSVs as the durable fix
reruns simulator.seed/materialize and validates the report

evals.prompt: Use the building-simulators skill to triage simulator.validate failures for an Okta identity simulator. The conformance report says: {"ok":false,"errors":["tool missing handler: submit_access_request_update","unknown allowed role: identity_admins","workflow handler collection missing from schema: approvals"]}. Provide the smallest ordered fix list and validation loop.
evals.expected_output: Maps each validation error to the corresponding pack artifact, adds or aligns the missing runtime handler/tool declaration, aligns allowedRoles with registry roles, adds the approvals collection or changes the workflow binding, reruns the smallest simulator validation command, and only proceeds once the conformance report has no errors.
assertions: fixes tools/runtime handler alignment
aligns allowedRoles with registry.json roles
repairs schema/workflows collection mismatch
orders fixes before rerunning validation
requires a clean conformance report before handoff

evals.prompt: Use the building-simulators skill to upgrade a Salesforce CRM simulator from a static fixture into a Fortune 500-grade upstream system for an account-risk agent. The agent must reason about stale account reads, opportunity approval, role-limited updates, campaign influence, external D&B risk signals, and auditability. Describe the simulator capabilities and artifacts expected before factory generation.
evals.expected_output: Chooses CRM as the primary archetype and acknowledges external_intelligence as an interlocked source where needed, proposes collections beyond accounts such as opportunities, activities, campaigns, approvals, audit_events, and external risk signals, includes stale reads/integration failures/role gates/state transitions, defines materialization and projection contracts, and requires validation/coverage before generating the agent workspace.
assertions: chooses crm as the main simulator archetype
models cross-system external intelligence without flattening it into vague rows
includes permissions, workflow state, failure modes, and audit trails
defines schema/tools/projection/materialization/workflows/seed expectations
runs simulator validation or coverage before factory generation
